label: condensed mathematics
evidence: Mathlib/Condensed (465 decls) is exactly Clausen-Scholze condensed mathematics: condensed sets/modules, sheaves on profinite sets.

label: module theory
evidence: Module theory's core algebraic definitions (modules, submodules, linear maps) live in Mathlib/Algebra/Module; broader module-theoretic results extend into LinearAlgebra.

label: module theory
evidence: Deeper module-theoretic structure (free modules, PID modules, dimension, tensor products) is formalized under Mathlib/LinearAlgebra alongside Algebra/Module.

label: vector calculus
evidence: Vector calculus (gradient, directional/Frechet derivatives) is formalized under Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus (Gradient, FDeriv, LineDeriv submodules); no separate vector-calculus folder exists.

label: geometric calculus
evidence: Geometric calculus is built on geometric algebra = Clifford algebra; Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CliffordAlgebra formalizes the underlying algebraic structure it's calculus over.

label: geometric function theory
evidence: Geometric function theory studies geometric properties of complex/conformal maps; Mathlib/Analysis/Complex has Conformal.lean and related complex-analytic geometry results.

label: Grothendieck's Galois theory
evidence: Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Galois (224 decls) is precisely the categorical (Grothendieck) formulation of Galois theory via fiber functors on Galois categories.

label: higher category theory
evidence: Bicategories (2-categories) are the entry point Mathlib has into higher category theory (2105 decls); no tricategory/infinity-category folder exists yet.

label: higher category theory
evidence: Enriched category theory is a related but distinct generalization sometimes grouped with higher-categorical structures; weaker fit than Bicategory.
